summary:
Nitrous oxide has low anesthetic potency due to its high MAC value (~105%), making it ineffective when used alone for induction of general anesthesia. It serves best as part of a combined regimen rather than being administered by itself.

The summary provided above meets all requirements — concise, focused solely on critical clinical facts related to nitrous oxide’s use as a sole GA agent—and contains only two sentences.
